score_note: Scores on 1-5 scale (not 1-10). Equivalent to ~9.0/10 when normalized.

issues.description: Examples are excerpts only; Write tool used without confirm step
No After This guidance
Grep tool missing from tools list despite using text search patterns
Write tool used without confirm step for architecture documents
Write/Bash used without confirm step; potential for unintended file creation
Uses Sonnet for system-wide deep analysis — Opus would be more appropriate for scoring 100+ prompts
Vague step verbs in sections 1-2; trigger precision could be improved
Body examples are TypeScript-only despite polyglot description; Go/Python examples missing
Only 1 worked example; complex agent needs at least 2-3 domain examples
Minor overlap boundary with kotlin-reviewer not documented
Overlap with security-reviewer for shell injection not disambiguated
Overlap with cpp-reviewer for C++ code with C headers not disambiguated
No exit criteria for partial audits (when only some commands have issues)
C++23 modules not mentioned despite being in claimed specialization
NoSQL patterns not covered despite description mentioning 'database' broadly
No example of what a passing vs failing audit looks like
No mention of go vet / staticcheck integration
Missing guidance on pprof CPU vs memory vs goroutine profiles
Coverage threshold (80%) hardcoded; should note it can be configured per project
No mention of Biome vs ESLint preference
Announce-but-not-confirm write pattern for README updates
Write without confirm on --recompute flag could overwrite existing reports
GraphQL schema breaking changes not fully covered
No guidance on when to escalate to architect agent
knip/depcheck/ts-prune only; no Go or Python equivalent tools mentioned
SSRF detection heuristics not described
No Agent tool listed despite being an orchestrator that delegates to specialist reviewers
Announce-but-not-confirm write pattern for go.mod updates
No Write tool in tools list despite generating eval documents
Sonnet may miss borderline quality cases; Opus recommended for --all mode
No guardrail documented for destructive Bash in delegated sub-agents

medium_severity_issues: prompt-quality-scorer: Sonnet instead of Opus for system-wide analysis
systemic_patterns: Announce-but-not-confirm write pattern appears in 4 agents (doc-updater, go-build-resolver, data-architect, e2e-runner)
Missing Grep tool in 2 agents that perform text search
Model selection: 3 agents use Sonnet for tasks that would benefit from Opus
Missing Agent tool in code-reviewer despite being an orchestrator
top_issues_summary: 0 HIGH issues. 7 MEDIUM issues, all related to: (1) Write/Bash used without confirm step in 4 agents creating potential for unintended side effects; (2) Missing tools in agent tool lists (Grep in feedback-analyst, Agent in code-reviewer, Write in product-evaluator); (3) Model selection — prompt-quality-scorer uses Sonnet for a task requiring deep analysis of 100+ items. Overall agent quality is high (avg 4.51/5.0). Primary improvement opportunity is standardizing the confirm-before-write pattern across all file-producing agents.
